elements.title: speckled spiny tree-rat
elements.contents: ... that of the three specimens used to describe the speckled spiny tree-rat (pictured), only part of a skull remains?

elements.title: Thomas Harvey
elements.contents: ... that Leeds Quaker and chemist Thomas Harvey manufactured the first commercially available small clinical thermometer?

elements.title: a Mexico City metro station
elements.contents: ... that in a Mexico City metro station, commuters can sing karaoke or play the piano?

elements.title: The Dark Pictures Anthology: Man of Medan
elements.contents: ... that the developers of The Dark Pictures Anthology: Man of Medan held judgment-free "death meetings" to brainstorm unique death scenes for the characters?

elements.title: a successful coup in New Caledonia
elements.contents: ... that an Australian warship backed a successful coup in New Caledonia in 1940?

elements.title: Jaime Felipa
elements.contents: ... that Jaime Felipa worked as an airport security guard at Curaçao International Airport during his judo career?

elements.title: a Manhattan townhouse
elements.contents: ... that the designer of a Manhattan townhouse "succeeded in almost completely Mediterraneanizing" the city block?

elements.title: one of the best goals
elements.contents: ... that Toluca FC scored what is widely regarded to be one of the best goals in Liga MX history?

elements.title: Laughing Jacobs Creek
elements.contents: ... that Laughing Jacobs Creek was reportedly named after an early settler's attempt to make a loon call?
